name: Bill Hardman
abstract: William Franklin Hardman Jr. (April 6, 1933 – December 6, 1990) was an American jazz trumpeter and flugelhornist who chiefly played hard bop.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Art Blakey and The Jazz Messengers/ Horace Silver Quintet/ Freddie Hubbard
Kool Jazz Festival: The Blakey Legacy

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: I joined the band. When I joined the band, McCoy Tyner was there. Joe Henderson, Bill Hardman, Junie Booth, and Art.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Griffin joined the Messengers, and it was Art Blakey and the Messengers, with Thelonious. Now I was supposed to play on part of it, and the Messengers -- Bill Hardman -- and I was out with Wilbur Campbell
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...Benny Golson and his Orchestra: Freddie Hubbard, Bill Hardman on trumpets... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. I didn't play on that one. I just did the writing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: me and Paul and Bill Hardman-- ... And me, Bill Hardman and Paul, man, cat on in town, man. And he taught--he taught--he taught me a lot, man, about this business

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Clifford Brown played with Chris Powell and the Blue Flames blues band, or Bill Hardman, played Tiny Bradshaw, Jimmy — you know, so most of those
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: Then teach Bill Hardman his notes. Then inevitably the next day when I’d come in and play... I learned a lot with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
